title: Sith lanvarok

{{Quote|If it has blood, you can make it bleed. If it has eyes, you can blind it. If it has a mouth, you can make it scream…|Text carved on a Sith lanvarok|<ref name="New Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology" />}}
The '''Sith lanvarok''' was a wrist-mounted projectile launcher that could fire a volley of metal discs, usually in a small but unpredictable spray pattern, which made dodging or blocking the deadly projectiles very difficult. Due to the [[lanvarok]]'s exceptionally long reload time, it was best used for delivering a one-shot surprise attack. To counter its lack of accuracy, a [[Sith/Legends|Sith]] could use [[The Force/Legends|the Force]] to guide the discs to their targets, turning the lanvarok into an incredibly deadly weapon.

Sith lanvaroks were typically designed to be exclusively right-handed or exclusively left-handed, the left-handed versions being based on the original lanvaroks of the [[Sith (species)/Legends|Sith species]]. Most were constructed of [[Durasteel/Legends|durasteel]], although rare versions did have a thin layer of [[Cortosis/Legends|cortosis]] armor to protect them from [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]] attacks. Since the weapon could be worn on the forearm, the wielder's hands were free to clutch a lightsaber or other [[Melee weapon/Legends|melee weapons]]. The lanvarok possessed a secondary trigger: a small nub that could be manipulated using the Force. A more primitive version of this weapon also existed, called [[Massassi lanvarok]].

==History==
Ancient legends suggested that the lanvarok originated as an effective hunting tool. Though quite common during the days of the [[Great Sith War/Legends|Great Sith War]], lanvaroks became extremely difficult to acquire after the rise of [[Darth Bane/Legends|Darth Bane]]. By the time of the [[Clone Wars/Legends|Clone Wars]] most lanvaroks were sold only as antique collector's items. However, a small number of [[Sith holocron/Legends|Sith holocrons]] contained detailed instructions for those eager enough to build their own.

In [[5 ABY/Legends|5 ABY]], [[Director of Imperial Intelligence]] [[Ysanne Isard]] discovered a Sith lanvarok which used to belong to [[Mara Jade Skywalker|Mara Jade]], and sent a left-handed agent to use it to kill [[Plumba]], an [[Imperial Ruling Council/Legends|Imperial Ruling Council]] member.
